title: Acronia quaternaria
taxonomy: Acronia quaternaria is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises a vast array of flowering plants, including coffee, gardenias, and some species of ornamental plants. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Acronia quaternaria is also classified as a member of the order Gentianales. Within this order, Acronia quaternaria is further classified along with other flowering plants that have similar characteristics. Additionally, it is classified as a genus within the subfamily Rubianoideae.

intro: Acronia quaternaria is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its demonstrated antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Acronia quaternaria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Acronia quaternaria is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations are used to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.
